title: zanthoxylum caribaeum subsp. nelsonii
intro: Zanthoxylum caribaeum subsp. nelsonii, also known as the Jamaican pepper tree, is a plant species belonging to the Rutaceae family, native to Jamaica and other Caribbean islands. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Zanthoxylum caribaeum subsp. nelsonii include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to prevent the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
